solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    vector<string> sortPeople(vector<string>& names, vector<int>& heights) {
        vector<pair<int, string>> people;
        for (int i = 0; i < names.size(); ++i) {
            people.push_back({heights[i], names[i]});
        }
        sort(people.rbegin(), people.rend());
        vector<string> result;
        for (const auto& p : people) {
            result.push_back(p.second);
        }
        return result;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n log n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution pairs each name with its corresponding height, sorts the pairs in descending order by height, and then extracts the names in this sorted order. The use of standard library functions ensures efficiency and clarity. The main strength is its simplicity and efficiency, while the only potential weakness is the use of extra space for the pairs.
